issues.display_title: Issue #15: Clean Up the Windows and Linux Menu Label
issues.main_description_html:
Clean Up the Windows and Linux Menu Label

Docs

Specs

app-version-placement.md

Plan

2026-06-24-app-version-placement.md

Context

Windows and Linux use the in-app menu bar. That menu currently includes the app version beside GoThru Photo Studio.

Goal

Remove the version from the Windows/Linux in-app menu label while keeping the menu itself unchanged.

What We're Building

A cleaner Windows/Linux menu row where the first menu label reads only GoThru Photo Studio.

File Involved

  • src/renderer/components/layouts/Header/WindowsMenuBar.tsx

Acceptance Criteria

  • The first menu label reads GoThru Photo Studio.
  • The version no longer appears in the menu row.
  • Existing menu items still appear and behave the same: File, Edit, View, Settings, Window, Help.
  • No About or update messaging is changed.

What Changes After This Task

Windows and Linux users see a cleaner menu row, while the version remains visible in the window title.

DEMO FROM DEVELOPER

MAC

issues.display_title: Issue #13: Define the App Name and Version Display Rules
issues.main_description_html:
Define the App Name and Version Display Rules

Docs

Specs

app-version-placement.md

Plan

2026-06-24-app-version-placement.md

Context

The app currently shows the version beside the first app/menu label. The intended behavior is to keep the menu label clean and move the version into the app/window title.

Goal

Create one clear rule for how the app name appears in menus and one clear rule for how the app title appears with the version.

What We're Building

A shared display rule where:

  • Menu label shows GoThru Photo Studio.
  • Window title shows GoThru Photo Studio <version>.

File Involved

  • src-tauri/src/commands/app_menu.rs

Acceptance Criteria

  • The menu label does not include any version number.
  • The window title format includes the current app version.
  • The version value comes from the app's existing version information.
  • Future version changes do not require manually editing menu text.

What Changes After This Task

The app has a single agreed rule for product name and version display, reducing the chance that different parts of the app show different labels.

DEMO FROM DEVELOPER

MAC

issues.display_title: Issue #14: Verify the Change Across Supported OSes
issues.main_description_html:
Result

QA status for branch feat/vscode-titlebar.

  • Windows: pending — to be captured locally (npm run tauri dev).
  • macOS: an initial run surfaced two issues that are now fixed:
    1. Traffic lights (close/minimize/fullscreen) were missing — caused by decorations: false, which removes the native buttons on macOS. Fixed by keeping decorations: true + titleBarStyle: Overlay on macOS and applying frameless only on Windows/Linux via runtime set_decorations(false).
    2. The duplicated app-name text in the in-window bar was removed and the version moved to the global menu (see #13).
      A fresh macOS screenshot should be captured to confirm.
  • Linux: pending / document as unavailable if no host.

Screenshots will be added to docs/migration/assets/app-version-placement-{windows,macos,linux}.png (or a note for any OS without a host), per the acceptance criteria.

Verify the Change Across Supported OSes

Docs

Specs

app-version-placement.md

Plan

2026-06-24-app-version-placement.md

Context

This is a cross-platform shell change. The result needs to be checked visually because app titles and menu labels are displayed differently across operating systems.

Goal

Confirm the app title and menu label meet the spec on Windows, macOS, and Linux, or clearly document any OS that could not be checked.

What We're Building

QA evidence that the version placement is correct and that the menu behavior did not regress.

File Involved

  • docs/migration/assets/app-version-placement-windows.png
  • docs/migration/assets/app-version-placement-macos.png
  • docs/migration/assets/app-version-placement-linux.png

Acceptance Criteria

  • Windows shows GoThru Photo Studio <version> as the window/taskbar title and GoThru Photo Studio in the menu row.
  • macOS shows GoThru Photo Studio as the app menu name and GoThru Photo Studio <version> where the OS displays the window title.
  • Linux shows GoThru Photo Studio <version> as the window title and no version in menu labels.
  • Screenshots are captured for each available OS.
  • Any unavailable OS is noted with a reason.

What Changes After This Task

The feature is ready for review with clear visual proof that the version moved from the menu label to the app/window title.

DEMO FROM DEVELOPER

issues.display_title: Issue #12: Apply the Versioned App Title at Startup
issues.main_description_html:
Result

Implemented on branch feat/vscode-titlebar.

The window title is set at startup to GoThru Photo Studio , sourced from package_info().version (i.e. tauri.conf.json version), so it tracks release bumps automatically — no metadata/installer/updater changes (src-tauri/src/lib.rs setup).
On macOS the version also rides the bold app name in the native global menu bar (see #13).
Acceptance met: title includes the current version on open, updates automatically on version bump, and product metadata/installer/update logic are untouched.

Apply the Versioned App Title at Startup

Docs

Specs

app-version-placement.md

Plan

2026-06-24-app-version-placement.md

Context

The app window needs to show the version in the title area, using the version already configured for the release.

Goal

Set the visible app/window title to include the current version whenever the app starts.

What We're Building

A startup behavior that displays the app title as GoThru Photo Studio <version>.

File Involved

Acceptance Criteria

  • When the app opens, the window title includes the current version.
  • The title updates automatically when the configured app version changes for a release.

What Changes After This Task

issues.display_title: Issue #24: Fix build ships cross-platform binaries (Win & Mac mixed)
issues.main_description_html:
Problem:
Builds ship binaries for all platforms. The Windows build also includes
Mac binaries, and the Mac build also includes Windows binaries. As a
result, installers get bigger and contain files that are not used on
that OS.

Expected:
The Windows build should only ship Windows binaries. The Mac build
should only ship Mac binaries.

Solution:
Separate the binary settings per platform so each build only picks up
the matching folder.

Acceptance criteria:

  • Windows installer contains Windows binaries only.
  • Mac installer contains Mac binaries only.
  • All features that use binaries (exiftool, enfuse, etc.) still work
    normally on each OS.
  • Installer size is reduced.

issues.display_title: Issue #17: Fix panorama switching when play pano on Preview
issues.main_description_html:
Fix Panorama Switching When Play Pano on Preview

Summary

When the user enables Play (auto-rotate slideshow) on a panorama in Preview, the app should automatically advance to the next image panorama after each rotation interval. .pts files and videos must be skipped during autoplay so the slideshow never stalls on non-viewable items.

Problem

Expected behavior: With Play enabled (layout.isAutoRotate), the viewer spins the current panorama, then advances to the next image file after rotationDuration seconds. .pts tour files and videos are bypassed. Autoplay stops when there are no more images ahead.

Current behavior:

Component Issue
Main.tsx autoplay timer Fires synthetic ArrowRight every rotationDuration ms via triggerRightArrow()
Preview.tsx keyboard nav getNextFile / getPreviousFile walk the full merged list [...images, ...regularFiles] with no skip logic
.pts files Listed in regularFiles (type: 'file'). Selecting one clears the viewer and shows the banner — slideshow appears stuck
Videos Listed in images (type: 'video'). Sphere autorotate is disabled for video (autorotate={!isVideo && layout.isAutoRotate}), but autoplay still lands on them and breaks the pano slideshow flow

User-visible symptoms:

  1. Slideshow stops on .pts — autoplay advances to a .pts file, viewer goes blank/banner, rotation interval keeps firing but no panorama is shown.
  2. Slideshow hits videos — autoplay lands on video files instead of continuing through image panoramas only.
  3. Toolbar next/prev during Play — left/right buttons dispatch the same ArrowRight/ArrowLeft events, so they share the same bug when autoplay is active.

Goals

Fix panorama file switching during Play so only image panoramas are visited.

Navigation logic

  • Add findNextImageFile / findPreviousImageFile helpers that scan the merged list and return only items with type === 'image'
  • When layout.isAutoRotate is true, ArrowRight / ArrowLeft (and D / A keys) use the skip helpers instead of getNextFile / getPreviousFile
  • When no next image exists, dispatch SET_AUTO_ROTATE with isAutoRotate: false (stop Play)
  • Manual navigation (Play off) unchanged — arrow keys still move through all file types including .pts and video

UI / UX

  • Play slideshow cycles through image panoramas only, respecting folder sort order (images + .pts interleaved in browse, but .pts skipped in slideshow)
  • Play stops cleanly at the last image (pause icon, no blank viewer)
  • Toolbar prev/next buttons behave correctly while Play is active

Out of scope

  • Changing manual (non-autoplay) keyboard or mouse navigation behavior
  • Autoplay for 360° videos as a separate slideshow mode
  • File Manager / Edit browse navigation

Technical flow

User clicks Play (isAutoRotate = true)
  → Main.tsx setInterval → triggerRightArrow()
    → synthetic keydown ArrowRight
      → Preview.tsx handleArrowKeys
        → [FIX] findNextImageFile(items, index)  // skip video + .pts
          → selectDisplayItem(next image)
  → Panorama autorotate on current image
  → repeat until no next image → SET_AUTO_ROTATE false

Key files:

  • src/renderer/components/features/Preview/Preview.tsx — arrow-key navigation, handleArrowKeys
  • src/renderer/components/features/Preview/Main.tsx — Play toggle, autoplay interval, triggerRightArrow / triggerLeftArrow

Test plan

  • Folder with mixed files: IMG_1.jpg, tour.pts, IMG_2.jpg, video.mp4, IMG_3.jpg (natural sort order)
  • Open IMG_1.jpg → enable Play → after interval advances to IMG_2.jpg (skips .pts)
  • Continue Play → advances to IMG_3.jpg (skips video)
  • On last image, Play stops automatically (no blank viewer, pause icon shown)
  • Disable Play → ArrowRight manually selects .pts and video as before
  • Toolbar next/prev while Play is on skips .pts and video the same way
  • Folder with only .pts and videos → Play stops immediately or does not advance to non-images
  • Regression: WASD / arrow grid jump (Up/Down) unchanged when Play is off

DEMO FROM DEVELOPER

WhatsApp.Video.2026-06-26.at.14.50.53.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #16: Fix Capture Photo on Preview
issues.main_description_html:
Fix Capture Photo on Preview

Summary

Capture Photo in Preview should export a flat (rectilinear) JPEG from the current 360° viewer angle using the bundled eq2photo binary. The renderer flow is already implemented, but the Tauri backend command is missing, so capture either fails silently or never becomes available in the UI.

Problem

Expected behavior (Electron parity): In Preview, when viewing a panorama in Normal mode, the camera button captures the current view and writes gothru_capture_<timestamp>.jpg to the active folder. The user sees loading state, success/failure toast, a log entry with preview link, and a processing-file row (uploadTo: 'capture').

Current behavior:

Layer Status
Renderer (Preview/Main.tsx) Implemented — handleCapture, FOV/angle conversion, toast, logs, processing UI
Bridge (electron-bridge.ts) Mapped — eq2photoCapture → eq2photo-capture
Binaries Present — src-tauri/binaries/mac/eq2photo, win/eq2photo.exe
Rust backend Missing — no eq2photo.rs, command not registered in lib.rs
IPC inventory Still unported — eq2photo-capture is one of the 2 remaining channels

User-visible symptoms:

  1. Capture button may not appear — button only renders when normal && isPanorama && !isVideo. Incorrect panorama detection hides it.
  2. Capture does nothing / fails — invoke('eq2photo_capture') hits an unregistered command; the bridge swallows the error and returns undefined.
  3. Silent no-op — handleCapture exits early if panoramaViewStateRef or panoramaViewSizeRef is still null (viewer state not yet reported).
  4. Angle parity risk — output must match Electron exactly: vertical→horizontal FOV, yawDeg = -(yaw - 90), pitchDeg = -(pitch - 90). Wrong convention = wrong crop.

Goals

Restore end-to-end Capture Photo in Preview with the same UX and output as the Electron app.

Backend

  • Register and implement eq2photo_capture Tauri command
  • Spawn bundled eq2photo binary with correct CLI args (--input, --output, --aspect, --fov, --yaw, --pitch, --roll 0, --quality max-detail)
  • Return { status, error?, outputPath? } matching Electron contract
  • Write output to {currentDirectory}/gothru_capture_<timestamp>.jpg

UI / UX

  • Camera button visible in Normal mode for panorama images (not video)
  • Loading state on button during capture
  • Success/failure toast + log entry with previewFilePath on success
  • Processing file row appears with uploadTo: 'capture'

Parity (severity: H)

  • Captured image matches the angle shown in the viewer (FOV/yaw/pitch convention unchanged)
  • Reference: docs/migration/features/preview.md — golden baseline capture_yaw30_pitch0.jpg

Docs

  • Mark eq2photo-capture as done in docs/migration/00-ipc-inventory.md
  • Update channel port count (165/166 or 166/166)

Out of scope

  • Image to Video frame capture (useImageToVideo.ts) — shares the same backend but needs its own parity verification
  • scale-up-image — still coming soon

Technical flow

User clicks Capture (Normal + panorama)
  → Preview/Main.handleCapture()
    → read fov/yaw/pitch from ViewInspect refs
    → convert angles for eq2photo
    → invoke eq2photo-capture
      → eq2photo.rs → spawn eq2photo binary
    → toast + log-manager + UPDATE_PROCCESSING_FILE

Test plan

  • Open an equirectangular panorama in Preview
  • Confirm camera button appears in Normal mode (not Flat / Little Planet / video)
  • Rotate view, click Capture — JPG created in current folder with expected timestamp name
  • Verify success toast and log entry with "View Result" / preview link
  • Open captured file — framing matches viewer at capture time
  • Test failure path (e.g. read-only folder) — error toast and failed processing row
  • Regression: non-panorama images still open in Flat mode; capture button stays hidden

DEMO FROM DEVELOPER

Screen.Recording.2026-06-26.at.13.59.48.mov

issues.display_title: Issue #1: Implement Recursive File Search Across Nested Sub-folders
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-26.17-08-46.mp4

Description

Currently, the search bar in the File Manager only performs a shallow search (searching exclusively within the immediate active directory).

Users working with heavily structured directories (e.g. Year > Month > Project ID > Pano > Enfuse) need to search for a specific file name recursively. If a user initiates a search at a high-level parent folder, the app must scan through all sub-folders regardless of depth (e.g. 1–5 levels deep) to locate the file.

Screenshot (user)

  1. "I am in this folder, and there are these sub-folders" (parent with several numbered sub-folders)
  2. "When I search, I want to search throughout all sub-folders and any level of sub-folder, like 1-2-3-4-5 depth" — current result: "No Search Result Found" for a file that exists deeper.
    (Drag-and-drop the annotated screenshot into the issue to embed.)

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (§3 File Manager & Preview). The File Manager already uses a real-time filesystem watcher (chokidar); recursive search should reuse main-process FS traversal and run off the UI thread so it doesn't freeze the app (cf. #1435 batch-freeze: heavy FS work must not block the UI).

Investigation directions

  • Make search a recursive directory walk from the active folder in the main process (depth-unbounded, with sane safeguards), streaming matches back to the renderer.
  • Async + responsive: run in the main process and stream/paginate results; don't build one giant in-memory list synchronously. Show progressive results + a loading state.
  • Cancelable + bounded: allow cancel; guard against symlink loops, huge trees, permission errors; skip unreadable dirs gracefully.
  • Consider a recursive toggle (this folder vs all sub-folders) and case-insensitive partial-name matching.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Searching for a specific file name from a parent directory successfully returns matching files located inside nested sub-folders.
  2. The search handles deep nesting (multiple folder levels) without crashing or timing out.
  3. The main UI remains fully responsive while the deep search is executing.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the feature · a deeply nested folder tree (e.g. Year > Month > Project > Pano > Enfuse, 4–5 levels) with known files at various depths.

TC1 — Recursive Match (primary)

  1. From a high-level parent folder, search a file name that exists several levels deep.
  2. Expected: The file is found and listed (with its location), not "No Search Result Found".

TC2 — Deep / Large Tree

  1. Search across a very large, deep tree.
  2. Expected: Completes without crash or indefinite hang; results appear (progressively if needed).

TC3 — UI Responsiveness

  1. While a deep search runs, interact with the app (navigate, click controls).
  2. Expected: UI stays responsive; search is cancelable.

TC4 — Edge Cases

  1. Search with no matches; search where some sub-folders are unreadable/permission-restricted.
  2. Expected: Clear "no results" state; restricted folders are skipped gracefully (no crash).

issues.display_title: Issue #25: Fix Issue Thumbnail Navigation Scroll Position Resets on Refresh
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-26.17-11-07.mp4

A UX issue has been reported in the newer version regarding the image thumbnails and the navigation section. When the thumbnails or navigation section refreshes, the user's scroll position is not preserved, and the view jumps back up to the top.

This creates a frustrating and unfriendly experience, especially when navigating through a large batch of stitched images.

Sub task:

  • Preview
  • File Manager
  • Edit

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The image thumbnails and navigation section can refresh or update state without resetting the user's scroll position.
  2. The user experience feels smooth and stable when interacting with the thumbnail navigation panel.

issues.display_title: Issue #27: Migration Binary — AI Image Gen
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Migration: ai-image-gen binary → native Rust

Migrate ai-image-gen.exe (source project: rust-image-staging) into the Tauri backend as in-process Rust, removing the external-process spawn used by virtual staging generate.

Branch (Development): migration/ai-image-gen-binary

Feasibility

Yes — the source is already pure Rust, and every runtime dep it needs (image, reqwest, base64, serde_json) is already in the backend. CLI-only deps (clap, indicatif, colored, gcp_auth) are not needed.

Scope

analyze and improve-description were already native (direct reqwest). Only generate still spawned the binary. Both spawn sites are now native:

Channel Was Now
room-staging-generate spawn ai-image-gen pano-edit --pipeline … ai_image_gen::run_pano_edit
virtual-staging-2d-generate spawn ai-image-gen --input … --image-type perspective ai_image_gen::run_generate_2d

New module src-tauri/src/ai_image_gen/: equirect.rs (warp/merge geometry, ported verbatim, now in-memory), strict.rs (prompt blocks — byte-identical text for output parity), providers.rs (Gemini + OpenAI, blocking reqwest), mod.rs (extract→AI→merge orchestration). Only the two paths the app used are ported; CLI-only modes (upscale, perspective, perspective-merge, single-step --boxes/--prompt, --extract-only, --rectilinear-from-equirect) are dropped.

Speed comparison (8000×4000 panorama, 1 region; native built --release)

The end-to-end generate is dominated by the AI network call (unchanged). Isolating the non-AI parts the migration changed:

Stage Binary (subprocess) Native (in-process)
Process spawn ~10 ms warm (~120 ms cold) eliminated
Extract (decode + warp) ~550 ms (writes max-compression PNG to disk) ~344 ms (in-memory)
Merge (32 MP) ~1425 ms (re-decodes from disk + re-encodes JPEG) ~724 ms (in-memory)
Geometry math only identical code identical
  • Geometry compute is the same algorithm/code → no algorithmic speedup.
  • Native removes per-call overhead: spawn, temp JSON/PNG/TXT writes, the extract-PNG disk round-trip between warp and the AI step, and redundant panorama decodes (~0.5–1.0 s per single-region generate, scaling with region count).
  • Also drops the ~8.8 MB binary from the bundle, removes the subprocess attack surface, leaves no temp files.

Follow-ups

  • Remove the now-unused ai-image-gen binary from repo + bundle config (verify tauri.conf.json first).
  • Update renderer dev-preview text in RoomStaging / VirtualStaging2D settings/utils/prompts.ts (cosmetic).### End-to-end parity test (real AI)
    Same input (1440×720 panorama), same 1-step pipeline, same key, same models (Gemini default gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview; OpenAI gpt-image-1). AI output is non-deterministic, so the check is functional parity (success/failure, preserved dimensions, comparable size).
Combo Binary Native Output dims Size (bin / native)
pano-edit · Gemini ✅ ✅ 1440×720 (= input) 232 / 235 KB
2D · Gemini ✅ ✅ 1440×720 (= input) 611 / 625 KB
pano-edit · OpenAI ✅ ✅ 1440×720 (= input) 229 / 229 KB
  • All four outcomes match. The three successes preserve the input dimensions exactly with near-identical sizes (delta = non-deterministic AI + JPEG q92).
  • 2D · OpenAI fails identically in both (error decoding response body: end of file) — pre-existing OpenAI /images/edits behavior (app feeds JPEG to a PNG-only endpoint), not a migration regression. The renderer always uses provider=google, so this path is parity-only.

End-to-end wall-clock (AI-dominated; large run-to-run variance at this small size): pano-edit·Gemini 14.6 s → 10.9 s · 2D·Gemini 11.5 s → 10.9 s · pano-edit·OpenAI 24.8 s → 28.7 s (binary → native). At 1440×720 the geometry/overhead delta (~0.5–1 s) is within AI jitter; it becomes visible at full 8000×4000 panoramas.

Result by developer

Migration: pano-level binary → native Rust (Issue #28)

Branch: `migration/pano-level-binary`

Summary

Migrates the bundled `pano-level.exe` (~31 MB, largest shipped binary) from an external process spawn to an in-process pure-Rust module. Implements the full HorizonNet `pano_lsd_align` algorithm in Rust.

Approach: Pure Rust (Approach A) — no C toolchain, no FFI, no OpenCV dependency.

Scope — Modules Ported

Rust file Source Responsibility
`pano_level/geometry.rs` geometry.cpp + types.hpp Vec2/Vec3/Mat3, trig, rodrigues, uv↔xyz
`pano_level/image_io.rs` image_io.cpp decode/encode, looks_equirectangular
`pano_level/projection.rs` pano_projection.cpp perspective tile extraction, ray↔pixel
`pano_level/lsd.rs` OpenCV lsd.cpp (Apache-2) Pure-Rust Line Segment Detector
`pano_level/detect.rs` line_detector.cpp per-tile LSD + spherical normals (rayon)
`pano_level/vp.rs` vp_estimator.cpp seeded RANSAC vertical VP + refine
`pano_level/confidence.rs` confidence.cpp confidence score + level
`pano_level/remap.rs` remap.cpp full-res panorama rotate (rayon bilinear)
`pano_level/mod.rs` leveling.cpp + main.cpp public API + pipeline orchestrator

Public API:

  • `level_panorama(input, output, opts) -> LevelReport` — apply leveling (Auto Level)
  • `estimate_metadata(input, opts) -> LevelReport` — dry-run, roll+pitch only (Image Straightener)

Integration — Call Sites Replaced

Call site Before After
`commands/auto_level.rs` `pano-level --input --output` spawn + pipe drain `pano_level::level_panorama()` direct call
`commands/binaries.rs::get_pano_level_metadata` `pano-level --dry-run --json -` spawn `pano_level::estimate_metadata()` via `spawn_blocking`

Both call sites: no more temp files, no pipe drain threads, no process exit code parsing.

Parity Bar

  • roll_deg / pitch_deg within ±0.1° of the binary on same panorama (tolerance, not bit-exact)
  • LSD is a reimplementation (pure Rust) — RANSAC weighted refine absorbs any segment-level differences
  • Apply output: same dimensions, visually equivalent leveling

Parity + Speed Results

Pending — run harness with real panoramas:

$env:PL_BIN="src-tauri/binaries/win/pano-level.exe"
$env:PL_PANOS="C:/path/a.jpg;C:/path/b.jpg"
cargo test --release --lib pano_level::validate -- --ignored --nocapture

Results doc: `docs/migration/pano-level-native-migration.md`

Key Technical Notes

  • LSD: Ported from OpenCV's Apache-2.0 `lsd.cpp` (not the original AGPL `lsd.c`). Found and fixed eigenvector bug during port: `atan2(λ₁ − mxx, mxy)` not `atan2(mxy, λ₁ − mxx)`.
  • RANSAC: `StdRng::seed_from_u64(1337)` (deterministic; not bit-identical to `mt19937(1337)`, absorbed by weighted refine).
  • Performance: rayon row-parallel remap + precomputed sin/cos tables; benchmark-driven JPEG encoder swap if encode-bound.
  • Cross-platform: Pure Rust, no platform-specific code → removes need for per-OS binaries.
